description_long: This object is a vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder, featuring a rectangular black metal body with a transparent dust cover and two prominent tape reels on top. The front panel is equipped with multiple rotary dials, switches, and indicator lights, as well as a large volume knob and a tape counter. A detachable wired microphone with a stand is connected to the recorder, suggesting its use for audio recording or playback. The side panels show ventilation grilles and various input/output ports, while the rear includes additional connection points and a power socket. The dust cover is partially open, revealing the tape mechanism and controls. The overall design is utilitarian, with a mix of metallic, plastic, and rubber components, and the brand 'SONY' is visible on the front panel.
description: A vintage black reel-to-reel tape recorder with a transparent dust cover, multiple control dials, and a wired microphone on a stand. The object features metallic and plastic construction with visible brand labeling and audio interface ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control panel with dials, switches, tape reels, and the attached microphone on its stand.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the microphone's cable, ventilation grilles, and the open dust cover lying to the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the transparent dust cover, tape reels, and the microphone positioned to the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional ports, the microphone stand, and the dust cover fully open.
